skill.name: requirement-translator
skill.description:
需求转译 - 将主观、模糊、口语化的用户需求翻译为结构化、精确的AI可执行规格。
This skill should be used when the user gives a vague, subjective, or ambiguous requirement,
or when the user explicitly requests requirement translation.
Triggers: 需求转译, 翻译需求, 明确需求, 细化需求, "帮我分析一下这个需求", "这个需求不够清楚"
skill.summary: Translates vague, subjective user requirements into structured, verifiable specifications that AI tools can execute precisely.
skill.author: Barry-Liu-888

security_audit.summary: All 6 static analysis findings were evaluated and confirmed as false positives. Markdown backticks (```) were misidentified as shell backtick execution. 'AC2' (Acceptance Criterion 2) triggered C2 keyword detection on a harmless specification template line. The substring 'des' in 'description' triggered weak-crypto pattern matching on YAML frontmatter. Chinese UTF-8 characters caused expected elevated entropy in bilingual content. The skill contains no executable code, no network access, and no file system operations — it is a pure instructional template.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Three backtick patterns in SKILL.md were flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution by the static analyzer. Lines 36 and 66 are Markdown code block delimiters (```), and line 78 is inline code formatting (`[推断]`). None represent shell command execution — they are standard Markdown syntax for a requirements specification template.

low_findings.title: False Positive: 'AC2' triggered C2 keyword detection
low_findings.description: The text 'AC2' on line 56 stands for 'Acceptance Criterion 2' in a numbered requirements specification template. The static analyzer matched it against C2 (Command and Control) malware infrastructure keywords. This is a harmless document template label, not an indicator of malicious content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: YAML 'description' field triggered weak-crypto detection
low_findings.description: The YAML frontmatter field name 'description' on line 3 contains the substring 'des', which the static analyzer matched against DES (Data Encryption Standard) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is the word 'description' used as standard YAML metadata, not a reference to encryption.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Bilingual UTF-8 text flagged as high entropy
low_findings.description: File entropy of 7.18 bits triggered an obfuscation heuristic. This entropy level is normal for mixed Chinese and English UTF-8 text. The file is legitimate bilingual documentation, not encrypted or obfuscated content.

content.user_title: Translate Vague Requirements into Clear AI Specifications
content.value_statement: Users often describe software requirements in vague, subjective terms that AI tools cannot execute precisely. This skill translates those ambiguous descriptions into structured, verifiable specifications with clear acceptance criteria and technical constraints.
actual_capabilities: Extracts core goals from vague or subjective user requirements
Identifies ambiguities, missing information, and hidden assumptions in requirements
Translates requirements into structured specifications with functional and non-functional sections
Generates objective acceptance criteria with verifiable conditions
Flags inferences with markers so users can review and correct them
Provides implementation recommendations with risk warnings and suggested execution order
limitations: Cannot gather missing information autonomously — asks questions when key details are absent
Does not execute or validate the translated requirements — it only produces specifications
Output quality depends on input quality — highly ambiguous requirements may yield incomplete translations
Limited to text-based requirement analysis — cannot interpret diagrams or visual mockups

use_cases.title: Refine a Vague Feature Request
use_cases.description: A developer receives an unclear request like 'make the login page better' and uses this skill to produce a structured specification with concrete design, security, and UX requirements.

use_cases.title: Bridge Communication Between Stakeholders and Developers
use_cases.description: A product manager captures stakeholder requirements in plain language and uses this skill to translate them into technical specifications that an engineering team can act on immediately.

use_cases.title: Prepare Project Requirements for AI-Assisted Implementation
use_cases.description: A student with a project idea translates their rough concept into clear, structured requirements that an AI coding assistant can understand and help implement step by step.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Requirement Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this requirement into a structured specification: [describe your requirement here]
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a simple, somewhat vague requirement and wants a basic structured translation

prompt_templates.title: Translation with Clarifying Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this requirement and identify all ambiguities, missing information, and hidden assumptions. List questions for clarification before producing the full specification: [describe your requirement here]
prompt_templates.scenario: The requirement is highly ambiguous and the user wants to clarify details before receiving a specification

prompt_templates.title: Full Specification with Risk Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a complete requirement specification including functional requirements, technical constraints, acceptance criteria, edge cases, risk analysis, and a recommended implementation order for: [describe your requirement here]
prompt_templates.scenario: The user needs a comprehensive, team-ready specification document

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Perspective Requirement Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this requirement from three perspectives: developer, designer, and end-user. For each, identify concerns, acceptance criteria, and potential conflicts between perspectives: [describe your requirement here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use case where requirements need multi-role alignment before implementation begins

output_examples.output:
## 需求规格书

### 1. 目标概述
优化仪表板加载性能，减少用户等待时间，改善用户体验。

### 2. 功能需求
- F1: 页面首次加载时间从当前状态降低至2秒以内 [推断]
- F2: 仪表板数据采用分页或懒加载方式逐步渲染
- F3: 添加加载进度指示器，避免白屏等待

### 3. 技术约束
- T1: 需保持现有数据准确性和实时性要求
- T2: 优化后不能影响其他页面的加载性能

### 4. 设计约束
- D1: 加载状态需与现有UI风格一致

### 5. 验收标准
- AC1: 页面首次内容渲染时间小于2秒
- AC2: 数据更新延迟不超过5秒
- AC3: 加载过程中无页面闪烁或布局跳动

### 6. 边界条件与异常处理
- E1: 网络超时时显示重试按钮和错误提示
- E2: 数据为空时显示空状态占位符

### 7. 优先级
- P0 必须: 首次加载时间优化至2秒内
- P1 应该: 添加加载进度指示器
- P2 可选: 预加载常用数据

推荐方案: 先分析当前性能瓶颈，再针对性优化数据查询和前端渲染。
风险提示: 懒加载可能影响SEO和用户快速滚动查找的体验。
实施顺序: 性能测量 → 后端查询优化 → 前端分页/懒加载 → 进度指示器

output_examples.input: Add a dark mode toggle to settings
output_examples.output:
## 需求规格书

### 1. 目标概述
在设置页面添加暗色模式切换功能，允许用户在亮色和暗色主题之间切换。

### 2. 功能需求
- F1: 设置页面添加暗色模式开关组件
- F2: 切换后即时应用主题变更，无需刷新页面
- F3: 用户偏好存储到本地，下次访问时自动应用
- F4: 系统级暗色模式检测，首次访问时跟随系统设置 [推断]

### 3. 技术约束
- T1: 需支持主流浏览器（Chrome、Firefox、Safari、Edge）
- T2: 主题切换不应产生页面闪烁
- T3: 需兼容现有的组件库和样式系统

### 4. 设计约束
- D1: 暗色主题需保持足够的文字对比度（WCAG AA标准）
- D2: 开关位置需符合常见设置页面的用户习惯

### 5. 验收标准
- AC1: 切换开关后0.3秒内完成主题变更
- AC2: 刷新页面后主题偏好保持不变
- AC3: 暗色模式下所有文字对比度满足WCAG AA标准

### 6. 边界条件与异常处理
- E1: localStorage不可用时降级为会话级主题切换
- E2: 系统主题变更时根据用户偏好决定是否自动跟随

### 7. 优先级
- P0 必须: 暗色模式开关及主题切换
- P1 应该: 偏好持久化存储
- P2 可选: 定时自动切换（日间亮色/夜间暗色）

推荐方案: 使用CSS变量实现主题切换，配合localStorage持久化。
风险提示: 第三方组件可能不支持主题变量，需逐一适配。
实施顺序: CSS变量定义 → 切换逻辑 → 持久化存储 → 系统偏好检测

best_practices: Provide as much context as possible — technical stack, target users, and constraints help produce more accurate translations
Review all inferred items carefully — they are educated guesses marked with [推断] and may need correction
Use the translated specification as a conversation starter, not a final contract — iterate with your team or AI assistant
anti_patterns: Do not skip reviewing inferred items — [推断] markers indicate assumptions that may be wrong and could lead to incorrect implementations
Do not treat the output as executable code — the specification describes what to build, not how to build it
Do not use for highly domain-specific requirements without providing domain context first — the translator needs background knowledge to produce accurate results

faq.question: What types of requirements work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Vague, subjective, or ambiguous feature requests work best. Examples include UI improvements, feature additions, and performance optimization requests where the user knows the goal but not the exact implementation details.

faq.question: Can this skill generate code from requirements?
faq.answer: No. This skill produces a structured specification document. You can then give that specification to an AI coding tool for implementation, but the skill itself does not write code.

faq.question: What does [推断] mean in the output?
faq.answer: [推断] marks information that the translator inferred from context rather than from explicit user input. Review these items carefully because they are educated guesses that may need correction.

faq.question: Is this skill only for Chinese-speaking users?
faq.answer: The skill was designed with Chinese-language output as the default, but it can translate requirements in any language. You can ask it to output specifications in English or other languages.

faq.question: How detailed should my original requirement be?
faq.answer: Even a single sentence works. The skill is designed to handle minimal input. However, more context about your project, technical stack, and constraints will produce a more accurate and complete translation.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It works with any AI tool that supports skill-based prompting and structured output generation.
